quotes: Language is the road map of a culture.
Mistakes are proof that you are trying.

blocks.text: We use Present Perfect for actions that happened at an unstated time in the past or actions that started in the past and continue to the present.

memory_sprint.prompt: Choose the correct form: She ____ to Paris three times.
We use 'since' for a period of time (e.g. 5 years).
memory_sprint.explain: False. We use 'for' for periods of time. 'Since' is used for a starting point (e.g. 2015).

gb_adaptive_quiz.q: Type the missing word: I have lived here ____ 10 years.
Write the Past Participle (V3) of the verb 'write'.

gb_why_chain.q: Why do we use Present Perfect when the exact time is not important?
reprompts: Are we focusing on WHEN it happened or WHAT the result is?

real_life.story: You are a tourist guide in Samarkand. A group of English-speaking tourists just arrived. You need to tell them about the places you have prepared for them.

q1.prompt: Translate: Men 2018-yildan beri bu yerda ishlayman. (Use Present Perfect)
q1.fb: Great! 'I have worked' or 'I have been working' are correct.

q2.prompt: Fill the gaps: We ___ (visit) the Registan square today.

q3.prompt: Ask the tourists: Siz hech osh yeganmisiz? (Have...)

q4.prompt: A tourist says they didn't try somsa yet. Say: Biz hali somsa yeganimiz yo'q. (We...)
q4.fb: Perfect use of 'yet' with negative Present Perfect.

q5.prompt: Write one more place in Samarkand you have visited using Present Perfect.
q5.fb: Good job applying the grammar to a real-life situation.

q6.prompt: What is the past participle of 'see'?

real_life.endSub: You guided the tourists perfectly using the Present Perfect tense.

boss_questions.q: Which sentence is correct? A) I have finished my homework yesterday. B) I finished my homework yesterday.
boss_questions.hint: Remember: if there is a specific past time (yesterday), we use Past Simple, not Present Perfect.

boss_questions.q: Write the correct form: My brother ____ (not / call) me yet.
boss_questions.hint: Use 'has' for third person singular, plus 'not', plus V3.

reflection.summary: Today you learned how to use the Present Perfect tense to talk about experiences and unstated past events.
reflection.question: What is the main difference between 'for' and 'since'?
reflection.spaced_rep: Review the irregular verbs list, focusing on the V3 column.
reflection.closing: Good job! Keep practicing your English.
